conditions: Chronic knee pain with intermittent swelling
Sleep disturbance related to rotating shifts
medications: Cetirizine 10 mg daily during allergy season
Fluticasone nasal spray (1–2 sprays per nostril daily as needed)
Naproxen 220–440 mg as needed for knee/shoulder pain (with food)
Melatonin 1–3 mg as needed for sleep (used sparingly)
health.mental_health: In recovery from an eating disorder; attends therapy and a support group. He can get anxious about body image and about being perceived as “difficult” when requesting workplace accommodations for mobility and fatigue, but has developed coping strategies and a structured relapse-prevention plan.
recent_visits: Physical therapy re-evaluation for knee strength and gait (last month)
Orthopedics follow-up for right shoulder instability and work restrictions (2 months ago)
Outpatient therapy session focused on relapse prevention and stress management (weekly)
Primary care visit for seasonal allergies and sleep issues related to shift work (3 months ago)

finance.debt: About $9,500 remaining in student loans from community college and a hospitality certificate; also carries a $1,800 balance on a credit card after paying for PT co-pays and a knee brace during a period of reduced hours.
recent_transactions: Purchase of a hinged knee brace ($129)
Groceries with higher-protein staples for recovery meal plan ($86)
Payment to therapist/telehealth platform after insurance ($35)

relationships: Jada Morris (girlfriend, grad student in public policy; helps him practice accommodation conversations and keeps an eye on recovery warning signs)
Marcus Bennett (older brother in Baltimore; checks in after flare-ups and sometimes drives him to appointments when the knee acts up)
Aisha Khan (close friend/coworker at the hotel; swaps shifts when his knee is inflamed)
DC adaptive sports community contact: Coach Luis Rivera (encourages low-impact training and social support)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly Instagram and TikTok; posts DC food spots, hotel lobby aesthetics, and occasional advocacy content about accessibility (without naming his employer). Keeps health and recovery details off public profiles; close friends list is used for more personal updates.
private_conversations: Discussing eating-disorder recovery milestones and relapse fears with his therapist and girlfriend
Talking with HR about ADA accommodations, documentation, and concerns about retaliation
Explaining to friends why he avoids certain gym culture/weight-talk situations
Venting about pain flare-ups and feeling judged by guests when he uses a knee sleeve or requests a chair at the desk

schedule.work_hours: Rotating shifts, typically 4 days/week: 7:00am–3:30pm or 3:00pm–11:30pm; occasional overnight coverage during staffing gaps. Requests limited consecutive standing hours and a sit-stand setup.
regular_appointments: Support group for eating-disorder recovery (Sundays 6:00pm, biweekly)
Seasonal allergy check-in/med refill (primary care, every 6 months)
upcoming_events: Meeting with HR and hotel GM to finalize accommodation plan (next Wednesday 2:00pm)
Orthopedic follow-up to review shoulder imaging and work restrictions (in 3 weeks)
Friend’s birthday dinner in Adams Morgan (Saturday evening)
Volunteer shift with a local accessibility advocacy org at a community event (next month)
schedule.daily_routine: Commutes by Metro, does a short PT home program in the morning or after work, preps simple meals aligned with his recovery plan, and tries to keep a consistent sleep window despite rotating shifts. Uses calendar reminders for meds, meals, and stretching breaks during desk shifts.

professional.company: A mid-size business hotel near Dupont Circle (part of a national chain with unionized departments but non-union front desk)
projects: Training two new front desk agents on check-in standards and de-escalation
Reducing chargeback/incident rates by tightening ID verification and folio notes
Coordinating with engineering to improve lobby accessibility flow (queue stanchions, clearer signage, and a lower counter position during peak)
Building a coverage plan to reduce last-minute overtime and stabilize shift rotations
professional.performance_issues: Has documented limitations on prolonged standing and lifting due to knee and shoulder injuries; occasionally needs brief seated breaks during peak periods. He is actively advocating for a sit-stand workstation and predictable breaks, and worries that some managers interpret this as lack of flexibility.
workplace_relationships: Supportive relationship with HR rep (Denise Caldwell) who is guiding the accommodations process
Tension with an assistant manager who prioritizes “always on your feet” optics during VIP arrivals
Good rapport with night audit team who appreciate his detailed handoff notes
Front office manager relies on him for guest recovery but sometimes schedules him too many consecutive high-traffic shifts

hobbies: Photography walks on the National Mall and along the Wharf (low-impact)
Cooking simple meal-prep recipes (air fryer and sheet-pan meals)
Podcasts about hospitality, urban policy, and disability rights
Light swimming and stationary biking as knee-friendly cardio
Attending live music at small venues when energy allows
habits: Wears a knee sleeve/brace on longer shifts and uses compression/ice after work
Keeps a structured meal schedule as part of eating-disorder recovery plan
Tracks pain and mobility in a notes app to support accommodation requests
Avoids high-impact sports and most pickup basketball due to ACL history
Uses air purifier and keeps windows closed during peak pollen days
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a room in a shared rowhouse in Columbia Heights with two roommates; chose it for Metro access and fewer stairs. Has requested small home accommodations like keeping common areas clutter-free to reduce trip risk and storing his brace/ice packs in a dedicated freezer bin.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Balanced, recovery-oriented eating with consistent meals and snacks; moderate caffeine, higher protein for PT days, and avoids restrictive dieting talk. Limits very spicy foods during stressful weeks because it can trigger GI discomfort.
